expected_investigation_steps.step: Identify that powershell.exe was spawned by winword.exe, an unusual parent-child relationship.
expected_investigation_steps.rationale: Office applications spawning script interpreters is a well-known initial-access/execution pattern.

expected_investigation_steps.step: Decode the base64-encoded PowerShell command line to inspect its behavior.
expected_investigation_steps.rationale: Encoded commands are commonly used to evade simple string-based detection.

expected_investigation_steps.step: Check the destination IP of the outbound connection against threat intel for known C2 infrastructure.
expected_investigation_steps.rationale: Confirms whether the PowerShell process is beaconing to an attacker-controlled server.

expected_containment_actions.action: Isolate host WKSTN-042 from the network.
expected_containment_actions.rationale: Prevents further C2 communication or lateral movement from the compromised host.
Mitigates risk of credential theft from the compromised session.
